front: Opera: contains the famous 'mad scene,' in which the title character sings the aria 'Il dolce suono'
Opera: the sextet 'Chi mi frena in tal momento' follows the title soprano being tricked into signing a marriage contract
Opera: the title character murders her husband Arturo after being tricked into a marriage contract
Opera: 'Ardon gli incensi' often ends with a flute cadenza by Mathilde Marchesi, as in Joan Sutherland's Met debut
Opera: based on Lucy Ashton, the title character of a Walter Scott novel
Opera: opens with Normanno searching for an intruder, who turns out to be Edgardo
Opera: ends with Edgardo killing himself after the title character's death

Opera: ends with the challenging soprano cabaletta 'Coppia iniqua'
Opera: the lead role is an example of a coloratura soprano, able to sing elaborate vocal lines with trills and arpeggios

front: Composer: Berlioz complained about this composer's prolific output: 'one can no longer speak of the opera houses of Paris, but only of [this man's] opera houses'
Composer: this composer's mentor was Simon Mayr
Composer: left Naples for Paris after censors refused to allow his production of Poliuto
Composer: Eugene Scribe wrote the libretto to this composer's Dom Sebastien
Composer: this composer's brother Giuseppe ('Donizetti Pasha') wrote the Ottoman Empire's first national anthem, the Mahmudiye Marsi
Composer: once conducted Rossini's Stabat Mater in Bologna
